_comment: Vertex words L2 for 2D Robinson tiling. Every interior vertex of a closed flat tiling has angles summing to exactly 360 degrees. Each entry is a MULTISET (sorted descending); cyclic ordering is not enforced in this prototype.
_format: list of multisets, each multiset = sorted descending list of angle degrees
_notes: These are all (p, q, r) non-negative integer solutions to p + 2q + 3r = 10.
12 multisets enumerated. Strict Penrose tilings use only a subset (the 7 canonical vertex types: Sun, Star, Ace, Deuce, Jack, Queen, King) and impose cyclic ordering rules.
For the first prototype we use the loose multiset legality only — any cyclic permutation is accepted.
This will admit non-Penrose periodic tilings (e.g. all-T or all-G patches). That is acceptable for the first WFC validation.
Penrose strictness can be added later by replacing each multiset entry with the explicit cyclic words it permits.

_legality_rules.open_vertex: word is a prefix of some entry if every angle in word, when sorted desc, matches a prefix of that entry's sorted-desc form AND the running sum does not exceed 360
_legality_rules.closed_vertex: word multiset equals some entry exactly AND running sum equals 360
_legality_rules.reject: word is not a prefix of any entry OR running sum exceeds 360
